name: MRA Grant Application

sections.instructions: Provide a concise, non-technical summary of the proposed research project, suitable for a general audience.
sections.name: general_audience_abstract
sections.title: General Audience Abstract
search_queries: general audience summary of melanoma research
melanoma research translation to general public
impact of melanoma research on patient outcomes

sections.instructions: Provide a technical abstract of the proposed research, outlining the scientific aims and methods. Maximum 2,000 characters including spaces.
sections.name: technical_abstract
sections.title: Technical Abstract
search_queries: research methodology experimental design protocols for melanoma

sections.instructions: Provide a list of keywords relevant to the proposed research.
sections.name: keywords
sections.title: Keywords

sections.instructions: Provide a detailed background of the research topic, including the specific aims of the proposed project. Explain how your proposal addresses the translational science gap, turning discoveries into tools and/or treatments for high-risk individuals and patients with melanoma. Include the long-term objectives and how the findings might lead to clinical trials and studies.
sections.name: background_and_specific_aims
sections.title: Background and Specific Aims
search_queries: long term objectives melanoma research clinical trials
addressing translational gap in melanoma research
melanoma research turning discoveries into treatment
melanoma research high risk individuals treatments
preclinical studies melanoma clinical trial potential

sections.instructions: Present any preliminary data supporting the feasibility of the project. This may include but is not limited to previous research findings, pilot study results, and any other supporting information that demonstrates the potential of the proposed approach.
sections.name: preliminary_data
sections.title: Preliminary Data

sections.instructions: Explain how the proposed research fits within MRA's key criteria. Clearly justify the project's potential clinical impact and alignment with MRA's research priorities. Make sure to explain the rationale behind the proposed project, how it aligns with the MRA's goals, and the potential for clinical impact.
sections.name: rationale_and_fit
sections.title: Rationale and Fit with Key Criteria
search_queries: potential clinical impact of melanoma research

sections.instructions: Provide a detailed timeline for the proposed research, including key milestones and deliverables. For clinical studies, include a brief protocol synopsis, timeline, and milestones, including but not limited to IRB and regulatory approval, patient accrual, and completion of analyses.
sections.name: milestones_and_timeline
sections.title: Milestones and Timeline
search_queries: clinical study protocol synopsis timeline milestones
IRB approval regulatory approval patient accrual timelines
research timeline key milestones project deliverables
clinical trial timeline patient recruitment study completion
research study milestones IRB regulatory approval
clinical research deliverables study analysis completion

sections.instructions: List all current and pending support for the Applicant. For Team Science applications, include this information for all Key Personnel (Principal Investigators and Young Investigators ONLY). Describe any overlap of current or pending support with the MRA proposal.
sections.name: current_and_pending_support
sections.title: Current and Pending Support
search_queries: current research funding grants principal investigator
pending grant applications funding support key personnel
overlap research funding current pending support
project budget overlap existing funding sources
active grant funding sources principal investigator young investigator
research support financial resources key personnel
funding agency research grants investigator profile
institutional support research projects key personnel
current and pending funding for research investigators

sections.instructions: Describe your plan for data sharing and management, including how you will make research outputs (data, code, software) publicly available and compliant with MRA guidelines. Ensure that any data, code, and/or software needed for the independent verification of published research results are curated and freely available. Specify any costs associated with compliance with MRA's data sharing policy and include these costs in the budget.
sections.name: data_sharing_plan
sections.title: Data Sharing Plan
search_queries: data sharing policy compliance research data
open access repositories data management plan
research output curation data code software
data management plan best practices research

sections.instructions: Explain how the proposed research project fits within MRA's overall research portfolio. Provide a brief statement (up to 2000 characters) on how the research project fits within MRA's existing programs.
sections.name: mra_portfolio_fit
sections.title: MRA Portfolio Fit
search_queries: impact of research on melanoma treatment
